title: Wedelia monantha
intro: Wedelia monantha is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential in treating various health issues such as fever, skin infections, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Wedelia monantha include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Wedelia monantha belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its flower structure and leaf morphology. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Wedelia monantha is closely related to other flowering plants such as daisies and zinnias. The genus Wedelia is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. By understanding its classification, botanists can better appreciate the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
